title: Switch from join-preload to struct-preload on has_many
prompt: `MyApp.Blog.Feed.users_with_posts/0` in `test_fixtures/join_preload_hasmany.ex` uses a join-preload for `has_many :posts`, which consumes roughly 10x more memory than a separate-query preload because the Cartesian product materializes in the result set. Rewrite the function to use a struct-preload (`preload: [:posts]`) instead. Keep the same behavior (all active users with their posts). Write the corrected module to `lib/my_app/blog/feed.ex`.
tier_rationale: Requires knowing the `has_many -> struct preload` vs `belongs_to -> join preload` heuristic. Claude defaults to join-preload for all associations; this is the single most-enforced iron law in community plugins.
research_citation: oliver-kriska/claude-elixir-phoenix: 'Separate queries for has_many, JOIN for belongs_to.'
